schemeSetu.description: Comprehensive collection of Central and Punjab Government schemes for age groups 6–45 years. Enhanced with required_documents, optional_documents, application_mode (online/offline), direct apply_link for online schemes, and step-by-step physical process for offline schemes.
schemeSetu.changelog: v5.0 — Added application_mode (online/offline) field; renamed apply_now_url to apply_link for online schemes; removed links for offline schemes; added precise physical process steps for offline schemes; consistent field structure across all 66 entries.

schemes.name: Samagra Shiksha Abhiyan
schemes.amount: Free textbooks + uniforms; transport allowance up to ₹6,000/year
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — enrollment open throughout academic year
schemes.description: An integrated umbrella scheme for school education from pre-primary to Class XII. Provides free books, uniforms, digital classrooms, and inclusive education support.

eligibility.residency: Indian students in government or government-aided schools
eligibility.otherCriteria: Special focus on marginalized groups, children with disabilities, and girls

financial: Free uniforms for girls and SC/ST (Class 1–8)

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to your nearest government school or government-aided school in your area.
Step 2: Meet the school principal or class teacher and ask for the enrollment form.
Step 3: Fill in the child's name, date of birth, and parent details on the form.
Step 4: Submit the birth certificate and Aadhaar card of the child at the school office.
Step 5: After enrollment, textbooks and uniforms are provided automatically by the school.
Step 6: For transport allowance, visit the District Education Office (DEO) with your documents and apply in person.

schemes.name: PM POSHAN Scheme (Mid-Day Meal)
schemes.amount: Free hot meal daily; ₹6.19/day (primary), ₹9.29/day (upper primary)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — benefit provided automatically on all school days
schemes.description: Provides free hot-cooked nutritious meals to government school students daily to improve nutrition and boost school attendance.

eligibility.residency: Students in government or government-aided schools
eligibility.otherCriteria: Also covers Madrasas under Samagra Shiksha

financial: Free hot meal every school day

how_to_apply: Step 1: Enroll your child in a government or government-aided school in your area.
Step 2: No separate application is needed — the school automatically provides a hot cooked meal to all enrolled students on every school day.
Step 3: If meals are not being provided, report to the Head Teacher or Block Education Officer (BEO) of your district.

schemes.name: Beti Bachao Beti Padhao (BBBP)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemes.amount: Linked to SSY — 8.2% interest/year; min deposit ₹250/year
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — accounts can be opened anytime for girls below 10 years
schemes.description: A national campaign to address the declining Child Sex Ratio and promote the education and empowerment of the girl child by preventing female foeticide.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Special focus on districts with low child sex ratio

financial: Linked to Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana with 8.2% interest per annum
nonFinancial: Support for girl child education enrollment

how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit the nearest Post Office or an authorized bank branch (SBI, PNB, Canara Bank, etc.) in your area.
Step 2: Ask for the Sukanya Samriddhi Account (SSY) opening form at the counter.
Step 3: Fill in the girl child's full name, date of birth, and parent or guardian details.
Step 4: Submit the filled form along with required documents and a minimum deposit of ₹250.
Step 5: Collect the passbook issued by the bank or post office — keep it safe for future deposits and withdrawals.
optional_documents: PAN card of parent (for large deposits)

schemes.name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
schemes.amount: Min ₹250/year to max ₹1,50,000/year; 8.2% interest per annum
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — open for girls below 10 years at any time
schemes.description: A small savings scheme for the girl child offering one of the highest interest rates. Encourages parents to save for their daughter's education and marriage.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Maximum 2 accounts per family; exception for twins/triplets

how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit any post office or authorized bank branch (SBI, HDFC, PNB, etc.) near you.
Step 2: Ask for the SSY (Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana) account opening form.
Step 3: Fill in the girl child's name, date of birth, and guardian's details completely.
Step 4: Attach all required documents and deposit a minimum of ₹250 to open the account.
Step 5: Collect the passbook — it records all deposits and interest earned.

schemes.name: National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship (NMMS)
schemes.amount: ₹12,000/year (₹1,000/month) for Classes 9 to 12
schemes.deadline: Annual — NSP registration opens June; state exam typically Nov–Feb
schemes.description: A centrally sponsored scholarship for meritorious students from low-income families to reduce dropout at Class 8 and encourage continuation of education.

eligibility.otherCriteria: 55% in Class 7 (50% for SC/ST); family income below ₹3.5 lakh/year; government school

how_to_apply: Step 1: Open scholarships.gov.in on your phone or computer.
Step 2: Click on 'New Registration' and create your account using your Aadhaar and mobile number.
Step 3: Log in and select the NMMS Scholarship scheme.
Step 4: Fill in your school name, Class 7 marks, and family income details.
Step 5: Upload required documents and submit the form.
Step 6: Appear for the state-level NMMS exam conducted by your state's SCERT.
Step 7: If selected, scholarship is credited to your bank account every year.

schemes.name: National Scheme of Incentive to Girls for Secondary Education (NSIGSE)
schemes.amount: ₹3,000 fixed deposit (withdrawable with interest after Class 10 and age 18)
schemes.deadline: Annual — apply through school after passing Class 8
schemes.description: Provides financial incentive to SC/ST girl students passing Class 8 to encourage enrollment in secondary school.

eligibility.otherCriteria: SC/ST; passed Class 8; government school; unmarried

financial: ₹3,000 in FD; withdrawable with interest after age 18 and Class 10

how_to_apply: Step 1: After your daughter passes Class 8, approach the school principal and ask about the NSIGSE scheme.
Step 2: The school collects details of all eligible SC/ST girl students passing Class 8.
Step 3: School submits the list to the District Education Officer (DEO) for verification.
Step 4: District authorities confirm eligibility — no separate application needed from the student.
Step 5: A Fixed Deposit (FD) account is opened in the girl's name at a nationalized bank.
Step 6: The FD passbook is handed to the student — she can withdraw the amount after turning 18 and passing Class 10.

schemes.name: Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalaya (KGBV)
schemes.amount: Free residential education — accommodation, meals, uniforms, books (no cost to student)
schemes.deadline: Annual — admissions at start of academic year (April–June); contact Block Education Office
schemes.description: Residential schools for girls from marginalized communities in educationally backward blocks ensuring quality free education.

eligibility.otherCriteria: SC, ST, OBC, minority, BPL girls; dropout girls

how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit the nearest KGBV school or the Block Education Office (BEO) in your area.
Step 2: Ask for the KGBV admission form and fill it with the girl's personal and academic details.
Step 3: Submit the form with all required documents at the Block Education Office.
Step 4: District officials check eligibility and shortlist candidates based on criteria.
Step 5: Shortlisted girls are admitted to the residential school — all facilities (food, stay, books, uniforms) are provided free of cost.
required_documents: Aadhaar card of student and parent

schemes.name: Mission Poshan 2.0 (Saksham Anganwadi)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemes.amount: Free supplementary nutrition, health check-ups, and ECCE
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — register at Anganwadi Centre anytime
schemes.description: Integrated nutrition support for children 0–6 years and adolescent girls 14–18, delivered through Anganwadi Centres.

eligibility.ageLimit: Children 6 months to 6 years; Adolescent girls 14–18 years
eligibility.otherCriteria: Also covers pregnant and lactating mothers

nonFinancial: Iron, Vitamin B12, Folic Acid supplements

how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit the nearest Anganwadi Centre (AWC) in your village or ward.
Step 2: Meet the Anganwadi Worker (AWW) and ask to register your child.
Step 3: Provide the child's birth certificate and your Aadhaar card for registration.
Step 4: After registration, supplementary nutrition and health check-ups are automatically provided at the AWC.

schemes.name: PM CARES for Children
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemes.amount: ₹10 lakh corpus by age 18 + ₹20,000 school scholarship + ₹5 lakh health insurance
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply anytime at pmcaresforchildren.in
schemes.description: Comprehensive support for children who lost parents due to COVID-19, providing education, health insurance, and financial support until age 23.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Both parents/guardian died due to COVID-19 from March 11, 2020

how_to_apply: Step 1: Open the website pmcaresforchildren.in on your phone or computer.
Step 2: Click on 'Register as New Applicant' and create an account.
Step 3: Fill in the child's details — name, date of birth, and school.
Step 4: Upload the parent's death certificate (mentioning COVID-19) and child's Aadhaar.
Step 5: Submit the application online.
Step 6: The District Magistrate will verify your documents and approve the benefits.
required_documents: Death certificate of parent mentioning COVID-19

schemes.name: Right to Education (RTE) Act
schemes.amount: Free education in government schools; free 25% private school seat for EWS children
schemes.deadline: Annual — RTE private school admission season is typically March–May
schemes.description: Guarantees free and compulsory education to all children aged 6–14. Mandates private schools to reserve 25% seats for EWS/disadvantaged children.

eligibility.otherCriteria: EWS/disadvantaged children for 25% private school quota

financial: Free education in private schools under 25% EWS quota

how_to_apply: Step 1: For government school — walk into the nearest government primary or upper primary school and request enrollment for your child.
Step 2: For the 25% EWS/disadvantaged private school quota — visit the District Education Office (DEO) or District Collector's office in your area.
Step 3: Collect the RTE admission form from the DEO and fill in your child's details and the list of preferred private schools.
Step 4: Submit the form with income certificate and address proof at the DEO office before the deadline (typically March–May).
Step 5: A lottery or selection process is conducted by the DEO; admitted children receive education completely free of charge in the private school.
required_documents: Aadhaar card of child and parent

schemes.name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC/ST/OBC Students
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Social Justice / Tribal Affairs
schemes.amount: ₹225/month (day scholar) or ₹525/month (hosteller) for Classes 9–10
schemes.deadline: Annual — apply on NSP by October 31 each year
schemes.description: Scholarship for SC/ST/OBC students in Classes 9 and 10 to reduce dropout rates at the secondary level.

eligibility.otherCriteria: SC/ST/OBC; family income below ₹2.5 lakh/year; government school

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to scholarships.gov.in and click 'New Registration'.
Step 2: Register with your Aadhaar and mobile number.
Step 3: Log in and select your category (SC/ST/OBC) scholarship scheme.
Step 4: Fill in your school, class, and income details.
Step 5: Upload caste certificate, income certificate, and mark sheet.
Step 6: Submit the form — your school will verify it.
Step 7: After approval, scholarship amount is credited to your bank account.

schemes.name: Balika Samriddhi Yojana (BSY)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemes.amount: ₹500 at birth + scholarships ₹300 to ₹1,000/year (Class 1 to 10)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply within 1 year of birth at nearest Anganwadi Centre
schemes.description: Supports girl children from BPL families with a birth grant and annual scholarships for education up to Class 10.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Maximum 2 girls per family; must be enrolled in school

how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit the nearest Anganwadi Centre (AWC) or ICDS (Integrated Child Development Services) office after the girl's birth.
Step 2: Ask the Anganwadi Worker (AWW) for the Balika Samriddhi Yojana (BSY) application form.
Step 3: Fill in the family's BPL details and the girl's date of birth.
Step 4: Submit the form with required documents for verification by the AWW.
Step 5: A post office or bank account is opened in the girl's name; the ₹500 birth grant is credited.
Step 6: Annual scholarships (Class 1 to Class 10) are credited based on school attendance — ensure school enrollment is updated at the AWC.
required_documents: BPL ration card or income certificate

schemes.name: NPS Vatsalya Scheme
schemes.amount: Contribution-based; min ₹1,000/year; grows via compounding until age 18
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — open at any PFRDA-registered Point of Presence anytime
schemes.description: A pension savings scheme for minors allowing parents to open and contribute to an NPS account until the child turns 18.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Managed by parent or legal guardian

financial: Tax benefits similar to regular NPS
Converts to regular NPS at age 18

how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit any PFRDA-registered bank branch or post office (Point of Presence / PoP) near you.
Step 2: Ask for the NPS Vatsalya account opening form for your minor child.
Step 3: Fill in the minor child's details (name, date of birth) and the parent or guardian's details.
Step 4: Submit all required documents and make the minimum initial contribution of ₹1,000.
Step 5: Receive the child's PRAN (Permanent Retirement Account Number) and passbook.
Step 6: Continue contributing annually at the bank or post office until the child turns 18.
optional_documents: Guardian's address proof (if different from Aadhaar)

schemes.name: Bebe Nanki Laadli Beti Kalyan Scheme
schemes.ministry: Department of Social Security and Women & Child Development, Punjab
schemes.amount: ₹20,000 deposited with LIC; total payout of ₹61,000 across 5 milestones up to age 18
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply within 1 year of girl's birth at nearest Anganwadi or DSS office
schemes.description: Punjab scheme to curb female foeticide by providing tiered financial assistance to girl children from birth to age 18 through LIC-backed disbursement.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Includes abandoned girls in Punjab orphanages born after Jan 1, 2011

financial: Total ₹61,000 disbursed at milestones: birth, school admission, Class 8, Class 10, age 18

how_to_apply: Step 1: Within 1 year of the girl's birth, visit the nearest Anganwadi Centre or District Social Security Office in Punjab.
Step 2: Ask for the Bebe Nanki Laadli Beti Kalyan Scheme application form.
Step 3: Fill in the girl child's name, date of birth, and parents' details.
Step 4: Submit the form with required documents — a DSS official will verify your application.
Step 5: After approval, ₹20,000 is deposited with LIC of India in the girl's name.
Step 6: LIC automatically disburses instalments at five milestones: birth, Class 1 admission, Class 8, Class 10, and age 18.

schemes.name: Mai Bhago Vidya Scheme (Free Bicycle)
schemes.ministry: Department of Social Security and Women & Child Development, Punjab
schemes.amount: 1 free bicycle per eligible girl student
schemes.deadline: Annual — distribution at start of academic year (April–June) through school
schemes.description: Encourages girl students in Classes 9–12 in Punjab government schools by providing free bicycles to reduce commute barriers.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Enrolled in government school in Punjab

nonFinancial: Free bicycle per eligible girl student

how_to_apply: Step 1: No separate application is needed — the process is managed entirely through your school.
Step 2: The school prepares a list of all eligible girl students in Classes 9–12 enrolled in Punjab government schools.
Step 3: The list is submitted by the school to the District Education Office (DEO).
Step 4: DEO approves and sends bicycles to the school — you simply collect your bicycle from your school.

schemes.name: Punjab Ashirwad (Shagun) Scheme
schemes.ministry: Department of Social Security and Women & Child Development, Punjab
schemes.amount: ₹51,000 one-time financial assistance per girl for marriage
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply before marriage date at District Social Security Office
schemes.description: Provides ₹51,000 to EWS families for the marriage of their girl child to reduce financial burden.

eligibility.ageLimit: Girl must be 18 years or above at marriage
eligibility.otherCriteria: BPL/SC/ST/OBC families; widows/divorced women for daughters; orphan girls

how_to_apply: Step 1: Before the marriage date, visit the District Social Security Office in your district in Punjab.
Step 2: Ask the office for the Punjab Ashirwad (Shagun) Scheme application form.
Step 3: Fill in the girl's age, family income details, and the proposed marriage date.
Step 4: Submit the form with all required documents for official verification.
Step 5: After approval by the DSS Officer, ₹51,000 is transferred directly to the applicant's bank account.
required_documents: Aadhaar card of girl and parents

schemes.name: Scholarship for Students with Disabilities (Punjab)
schemes.ministry: Department of Social Security and Women & Child Development, Punjab
schemes.amount: ₹200/month (Class 1–8); ₹300/month (Class 9 and above)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply at District Social Security Office anytime during academic year
schemes.description: 100% state-funded monthly scholarship for students with disabilities in Punjab schools.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Recognized disability certificate; enrolled in Punjab school

how_to_apply: Step 1: Obtain a disability certificate from the Chief Medical Officer (CMO) in your district in Punjab.
Step 2: Visit the District Social Security Office in Punjab with all required documents.
Step 3: Ask for the Scholarship for Students with Disabilities application form and fill it completely.
Step 4: Submit the form with disability certificate, school certificate, and bank account details.
Step 5: After verification by the DSS officer, monthly scholarship is credited directly to your bank account (₹200/month for Classes 1–8; ₹300/month for Class 9 and above).
required_documents: Disability certificate from CMO or authorized medical authority
Aadhaar card of student and parent

schemes.name: PM YASASVI Scholarship (OBC/EBC/DNT)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.deadline: Annual — YET exam registration opens around July each year at yet.nta.ac.in
schemes.description: One of India's highest school-level scholarships for OBC/EBC/DNT students in Classes 9 and 11, requiring qualifying the YASASVI Entrance Test (YET).

eligibility.otherCriteria: OBC/EBC/DNT; family income below ₹2.5 lakh/year; must qualify YET by NTA

how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit yet.nta.ac.in when YET registrations open (usually July).
Step 2: Register with your Aadhaar and fill in your class and category details.
Step 3: Pay the exam registration fee (if any) and download the admit card.
Step 4: Appear for the YASASVI Entrance Test (YET).
Step 5: After results, go to scholarships.gov.in and apply with your YET score and documents.
Step 6: Scholarship is credited to your bank account after selection.

schemes.name: AICTE Swanath Scholarship
schemes.amount: ₹50,000/year for the full course duration (max 4 years degree / 3 years diploma)
schemes.deadline: Annual — applications open August–October on AICTE portal
schemes.description: Supports orphan students, COVID-19 orphans, and wards of martyred armed forces pursuing technical education in AICTE-approved institutions.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Orphan/COVID-19 orphan/ward of martyred armed forces; income below ₹8 lakh/year

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to aicte-india.org and find the Swanath Scholarship section.
Step 2: Click on 'Apply Now' and register using your Aadhaar and email.
Step 3: Fill in your personal, academic, and institution details.
Step 4: Upload orphan proof / COVID death certificate / armed forces martyr certificate.
Step 5: Submit the form — your institution will verify your enrollment.
Step 6: AICTE releases ₹50,000 annually directly to your bank account.
optional_documents: Death certificate of parent mentioning COVID-19 (for COVID orphans)

schemes.name: Punjab Chief Minister's Scholarship Scheme
schemes.amount: Tuition fee waiver + monthly stipend (varies by education level)
schemes.deadline: Annual — apply on scholarships.punjab.gov.in; deadline typically October 31
schemes.description: Financial assistance and tuition fee waiver for meritorious EWS students in Punjab government institutions.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Meritorious EWS students in government institutions in Punjab

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to scholarships.punjab.gov.in and click on 'Student Registration'.
Step 2: Register using your Aadhaar and mobile number.
Step 3: Log in and fill in your personal and academic details.
Step 4: Upload income certificate, mark sheets, and domicile certificate.
Step 5: Submit the form — your school or college will verify it.
Step 6: Approved scholarship is credited to your bank account.

schemes.name: INSPIRE Scholarship (Innovation in Science Pursuit for Inspired Research)
schemes.amount: ₹80,000/year (₹60,000 scholarship + ₹20,000 mentorship grant)
schemes.deadline: Annual — apply after Class 12 results; deadline typically September–November each year
schemes.description: Awarded to the top 1% students in Class 12 board exams across all boards. Encourages meritorious students to pursue Natural and Basic Sciences at undergraduate and postgraduate level.

eligibility.ageLimit: Students in first year of BSc / BS / B.Stat / B.Math / Int. MSc / Int. MS programs
eligibility.otherCriteria: Must be in top 1% of their Class 12 board; must pursue natural/basic science at UG level; age below 22 years at time of application

how_to_apply: Step 1: Check if you are in the top 1% of your Class 12 board exam results.
Step 2: Take admission in a BSc/BS/B.Stat/B.Math or Integrated MSc/MS program in Natural Sciences.
Step 3: Go to online-inspire.gov.in and register as a new applicant.
Step 4: Fill in your Class 12 rank details and institution admission proof.
Step 5: Upload required documents and submit the application.
Step 6: After verification by DST, scholarship is credited to your bank account annually.
required_documents: Class 12 mark sheet showing top 1% rank
optional_documents: Caste certificate (for SC/ST quota), if applicable

schemes.name: CBSE Merit Scholarship for Single Girl Child (Class X)
schemes.amount: ₹500/month (₹6,000/year) for Classes 11 and 12
schemes.deadline: Annual — applications open September–November each year at cbse.gov.in
schemes.description: Awarded to meritorious single girl children who are the only child in their family and have passed CBSE Class X with high marks, to encourage them to continue secondary education.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Only child of parents (no siblings); passed CBSE Class X with 60% or above; studying in CBSE-affiliated school in Class 11/12

financial: Renewable in Class 12 on maintaining 50% marks

how_to_apply: Step 1: After receiving your CBSE Class X result, visit cbse.gov.in and download the annual merit scholarship notification and form from the 'Scholarships' section.
Step 2: Fill in the application form with your Class X roll number, marks (60% or above), and family/school details.
Step 3: Get the completed form attested by your school principal with the school seal.
Step 4: Attach all required documents and submit the application to CBSE's regional office as per the notification address.
Step 5: CBSE reviews all applications and credits the scholarship (₹500/month) to your bank account.
required_documents: CBSE Class X mark sheet (60% or above)
Affidavit from parent that student is the only child
optional_documents: Previous year scholarship award letter (for renewal)

schemes.name: Top Class Education Scheme for SC Students
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.amount: Full tuition fee + ₹2,220/month (hosteller) or ₹1,500/month (day scholar) maintenance allowance
schemes.deadline: Annual — apply on NSP by October 31 each year
schemes.description: Provides comprehensive financial support to meritorious SC students who have secured admission in top institutions in India (IITs, IIMs, NLUs, top engineering/medical colleges) after Class 12.

eligibility.ageLimit: Students in top institutions (UG and PG level)
eligibility.otherCriteria: Scheduled Caste; admission in notified top-class institutions (IITs, NITs, IIMs, NLUs, etc.); family income below ₹8 lakh/year

how_to_apply: Step 1: Secure admission in a notified top institution (IIT, NIT, IIM, NLU, AIIMS, etc.).
Step 2: Go to scholarships.gov.in and click 'New Registration'.
Step 3: Register and log in with your Aadhaar.
Step 4: Select 'Top Class Education Scheme for SC Students' and fill the form.
Step 5: Upload caste certificate, income certificate, institution admission letter.
Step 6: Institution verifies your admission — scholarship is released to your bank account.
required_documents: Admission letter from notified top institution

schemes.name: UGC Post-Graduate Merit Scholarship for University Rank Holders
schemes.ministry: University Grants Commission (UGC) / Ministry of Education
schemes.amount: ₹3,100/month for 2 years (full PG course duration)
schemes.deadline: Annual — apply on NSP; check ugc.ac.in for current session dates
schemes.description: Awarded to students who secured 1st or 2nd rank at the UG level and are pursuing a regular full-time non-professional postgraduate course in India.

eligibility.ageLimit: Below 30 years at time of PG admission
eligibility.otherCriteria: 1st or 2nd rank at UG level; pursuing full-time PG course in recognized university; scored above 60% in UG; not in professional PG (MBA/MCA/LLM/etc.)

how_to_apply: Step 1: After securing 1st or 2nd rank in your UG exam, take admission in a full-time PG course.
Step 2: Go to scholarships.gov.in and register as a new user.
Step 3: Select the UGC PG Merit Scholarship scheme and fill the application.
Step 4: Upload your UG rank certificate, PG admission letter, and mark sheets.
Step 5: Your university verifies the application.
Step 6: Scholarship of ₹3,100/month is credited to your bank account.
required_documents: UG rank certificate (1st or 2nd rank proof)
PG admission letter from recognized university

schemes.name: Dr. Ambedkar Post-Matric Scholarship for BC/OBC Students (Punjab)
schemes.ministry: Department of Welfare of SC/BC, Punjab
schemes.amount: Full tuition fee reimbursement + ₹1,000–₹1,700/month maintenance allowance
schemes.deadline: Annual — apply on scholarships.punjab.gov.in; deadline typically October 31 each year
schemes.description: Provides post-matric financial assistance to students from Backward Classes (BC) and OBC categories in Punjab to pursue higher education after Class 10.

eligibility.otherCriteria: BC/OBC category; family income below ₹2.5 lakh/year; enrolled in recognized institution

financial: ₹1,000–₹1,700/month maintenance allowance (varies by level and hosteller/day scholar)

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to scholarships.punjab.gov.in and click 'Student Registration'.
Step 2: Register with your Aadhaar and fill in your details.
Step 3: Select the Dr. Ambedkar Post-Matric Scholarship for BC scheme.
Step 4: Enter your academic, income, and institution details.
Step 5: Upload required documents.
Step 6: Your institution verifies the form — scholarship is credited to your bank account.

schemes.name: Punjab Post-Matric Scholarship for SC Students
schemes.ministry: Department of Welfare of SC/BC, Punjab
schemes.amount: Full tuition fee + ₹1,200–₹2,250/month maintenance allowance
schemes.deadline: Annual — apply on scholarships.punjab.gov.in by October 31 each year
schemes.description: State-funded scholarship for SC students in Punjab pursuing education from Class 11 onwards, covering full tuition and living expenses.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Scheduled Caste; family income below ₹2.5 lakh/year; enrolled in recognized institution in Punjab

financial: Book allowance and study tour charges

how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit scholarships.punjab.gov.in and register as a new student.
Step 2: Log in and select Punjab SC Post-Matric Scholarship.
Step 3: Fill in your class, institution, and family income details.
Step 4: Upload your SC caste certificate, income certificate, and mark sheets.
Step 5: Submit and get institution verification done.
Step 6: Scholarship is credited to your Aadhaar-linked bank account.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY 4.0)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
schemes.amount: Free skill training + average monetary reward of ₹8,000 upon certification
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — rolling admissions at PMKVY Training Centres throughout the year
schemes.description: India's flagship free skill training scheme for youth to improve employability with industry-relevant certification and placement support.

eligibility.otherCriteria: School/college dropouts and unemployed youth given priority

nonFinancial: Soft skills and digital literacy training

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to skillindiadigital.gov.in or call 1800-123-9626.
Step 2: Search for a PMKVY Training Centre near your location.
Step 3: Visit the training centre and fill the registration form.
Step 4: Submit Aadhaar and bank details for registration.
Step 5: Complete the skill training program (3–6 months).
Step 6: Appear for the final assessment exam.
Step 7: Receive your NSDC skill certificate and monetary reward.

schemes.name: PM Internship Scheme (PMIS)
schemes.amount: ₹5,000/month allowance + ₹6,000 one-time assistance
schemes.deadline: Annual rounds — check pminternship.mca.gov.in for current round dates
schemes.description: Provides 1 crore youth with 12-month internships in top 500 Indian companies with monthly allowance and hands-on industry experience.

eligibility.residency: Indian citizen; not in full-time education
eligibility.otherCriteria: ITI/diploma/BA/BSc/BCom graduates; family income below ₹8 lakh; no IIT/IIM/NLU degree

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to pminternship.mca.gov.in and click 'Register as Candidate'.
Step 2: Create your account using Aadhaar and email ID.
Step 3: Fill in your educational qualification and skill details.
Step 4: Browse available internship listings from top 500 companies.
Step 5: Apply to internships that match your qualification.
Step 6: Attend company interview and get selected.
Step 7: Complete 12-month internship and receive monthly ₹5,000 allowance.

schemes.name: Startup India Initiative
schemes.amount: Tax exemption 3 years + 80% patent fee concession + access to ₹10,000 crore Fund of Funds
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply for DPIIT recognition anytime at startupindia.gov.in
schemes.description: India's flagship program for entrepreneurs providing recognition, tax benefits, funding, and regulatory simplification for startups.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Startup less than 10 years old; turnover below ₹100 crore; scalable innovative business

financial: Tax exemption on profits for 3 years (Section 80-IAC)
Capital gains tax exemption for investors
Access to ₹10,000 crore Fund of Funds

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to startupindia.gov.in and click 'Register'.
Step 2: Create your account and fill in founder and startup details.
Step 3: Describe your innovative product or service briefly.
Step 4: Upload company incorporation certificate (Pvt Ltd / LLP / Partnership).
Step 5: Submit the application for DPIIT recognition.
Step 6: Receive DPIIT recognition certificate and access all startup benefits.
required_documents: Certificate of incorporation (Pvt Ltd / LLP / Partnership deed)

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY)
schemes.amount: Shishu: up to ₹50,000 | Kishore: ₹50,001–₹5 lakh | Tarun: ₹5–₹10 lakh | Tarun Plus: up to ₹20 lakh
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply at any bank, MFI, or NBFC anytime
schemes.description: Collateral-free micro-enterprise loans for non-farm income-generating activities for youth and entrepreneurs.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Non-farm micro/small enterprise; not a wilful defaulter

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to udyamimitra.in (online portal for Mudra loans).
Step 2: Click 'Apply Now' and register with Aadhaar and PAN.
Step 3: Select the loan category (Shishu/Kishore/Tarun) based on your need.
Step 4: Fill in your business details and required loan amount.
Step 5: Upload documents and submit the application.
Step 6: A bank/NBFC/MFI will review and sanction the loan.
Alternatively: Visit any nearby government bank and ask for a Mudra loan form.

schemes.name: Post-Matric Scholarship for SC Students (Central)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.amount: ₹380–₹1,200/month maintenance allowance + full tuition fee reimbursement
schemes.deadline: Annual — apply on NSP by October 31 each year
schemes.description: Financial assistance to SC students beyond Class 10 in recognized institutions to enable completion of higher education.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Scheduled Caste; family income below ₹2.5 lakh/year

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to scholarships.gov.in and click 'New Registration'.
Step 2: Register with Aadhaar and mobile number.
Step 3: Log in and select 'Post-Matric Scholarship for SC Students'.
Step 4: Fill in your institution, class, and bank details.
Step 5: Upload SC caste certificate, income certificate, and previous mark sheet.
Step 6: Your institution verifies the form.
Step 7: Scholarship is credited to your Aadhaar-linked bank account.

schemes.name: Central Sector Scholarship for College & University Students
schemes.amount: ₹12,000/year (first 3 years); ₹20,000/year (4th and 5th year)
schemes.deadline: Annual — apply on NSP; deadline October 31 each year
schemes.description: Merit-based scholarship for students above 80th percentile in Class 12 from low-income families pursuing higher education.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Above 80th percentile in Class 12; family income below ₹4.5 lakh; not availing other scholarships

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to scholarships.gov.in after your Class 12 result.
Step 2: Click 'New Registration' and register with Aadhaar.
Step 3: Select 'Central Sector Scheme of Scholarships for College Students'.
Step 4: Enter your Class 12 board name, roll number, and marks.
Step 5: Fill in your college admission and family income details.
Step 6: Upload documents and submit the application.
Step 7: College verifies — scholarship is credited to your bank account.

schemes.name: PM-Vidyalakshmi Education Loan Scheme
schemes.amount: Loan up to ₹10 lakh (collateral-free); 3% interest subvention for income below ₹8 lakh/year
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply anytime after college admission at vidyalakshmi.co.in
schemes.description: Centralized education loan portal providing collateral-free loans for quality higher education with government guarantee.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Admitted to QHEI; income below ₹8 lakh preferred; not availing other government schemes

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to vidyalakshmi.co.in and click 'Student Registration'.
Step 2: Register with your email and mobile number.
Step 3: Fill in your educational qualifications and college admission details.
Step 4: Browse available banks and apply to multiple banks at once.
Step 5: Upload your college fee structure, admission letter, and income proof.
Step 6: Selected bank contacts you to complete the loan process.
Step 7: Loan is disbursed directly to your institution.
optional_documents: Income certificate of parents (for interest subvention)

schemes.name: Agnipath / Agniveer Scheme
schemes.amount: ₹30,000–₹40,000/month salary + ₹11.71 lakh tax-free Seva Nidhi at exit
schemes.deadline: Annual recruitment cycles — check joinindianarmy.nic.in for active notifications
schemes.description: Short-term 4-year military service scheme for youth with financial benefits, skills, and 25% chance of permanent enrollment.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Must meet physical and educational requirements of respective service

financial: ₹11.71 lakh tax-free Seva Nidhi at exit

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to joinindianarmy.nic.in (Army), joinindiannavy.gov.in (Navy), or airmenselection.cdac.in (Air Force).
Step 2: Watch for active Agniveer recruitment notification.
Step 3: Click 'Apply Online' and fill in your personal and educational details.
Step 4: Upload required documents and submit the application.
Step 5: Download admit card and appear for written exam.
Step 6: Qualify physical fitness test and medical examination.
Step 7: Receive appointment letter and join training.
optional_documents: NCC certificate (if any, for bonus marks)

schemes.name: National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS)
schemes.amount: ₹8,000–₹9,000/month stipend (employer contribution + ₹1,500/month government under NAPS)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — register anytime at nats.education.gov.in; openings depend on company
schemes.description: Practical 1–2 year apprenticeship training for engineering/diploma graduates in government and private organizations.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Engineering or diploma graduates from AICTE-approved institutions

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to nats.education.gov.in and click 'Register' under 'Apprentice'.
Step 2: Create your account using Aadhaar and academic details.
Step 3: Search for apprenticeship openings by your trade or location.
Step 4: Apply to relevant company openings.
Step 5: Attend the company's interview or selection process.
Step 6: Sign the apprenticeship agreement — stipend begins from Day 1.
optional_documents: Date of birth proof (if not on degree)

schemes.name: National Youth Corps (NYC) Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports
schemes.amount: ₹5,000/month honorarium during volunteer service
schemes.deadline: Annual — contact nearest Nehru Yuva Kendra (NYK) for current enrollment cycle
schemes.description: Builds disciplined youth volunteers contributing to community development, disaster relief, and environmental awareness.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Unemployed; minimum matriculation qualification

nonFinancial: Service certificate for government job applications

how_to_apply: Step 1: Find the nearest Nehru Yuva Kendra (NYK) office in your district — there is one in every district of India.
Step 2: Visit the NYK office during working hours and request the National Youth Corps (NYC) application form.
Step 3: Fill in the form with your educational qualifications, contact details, and personal information.
Step 4: Submit the form along with all required documents at the NYK office.
Step 5: Attend the selection process organized by NYK — shortlisted volunteers are deployed in community service activities and paid ₹5,000/month honorarium.

schemes.name: Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP)
schemes.amount: 25–45% government subsidy; project up to ₹50 lakh (manufacturing), ₹20 lakh (services)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply anytime at kviconline.gov.in/pmegpeportal
schemes.description: Credit-linked subsidy scheme for setting up micro-enterprises in non-farm sectors with government margin money subsidy.

eligibility.otherCriteria: New enterprise only; minimum 8th standard pass for manufacturing above ₹10 lakh

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to kviconline.gov.in/pmegpeportal and click 'Online Application'.
Step 2: Register and fill in your personal details and business plan.
Step 3: Select your project category (manufacturing/service/trading).
Step 4: Submit application to KVIC/KVIB/DIC office.
Step 5: Attend interview conducted by the task force committee.
Step 6: If approved, bank sanctions the loan and government subsidy is credited.

schemes.name: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)
schemes.amount: Free training + placement with min ₹6,000/month salary guarantee
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — rolling admissions at DDU-GKY centres; contact DRDA
schemes.description: Free market-linked skill training for rural poor youth with mandatory placement support in the formal sector.

eligibility.ageLimit: 15 to 35 years (45 for women and vulnerable groups)
eligibility.otherCriteria: SECC 2011 families; priority to SC/ST, women, PWD

nonFinancial: Free accommodation and meals during training

how_to_apply: Step 1: Contact the nearest District Rural Development Agency (DRDA) office in your district or visit a DDU-GKY-approved training centre directly.
Step 2: Check at the Gram Panchayat office if your family is listed under SECC 2011.
Step 3: Fill the DDU-GKY registration form at the training centre.
Step 4: Complete the skill training program (3–12 months) — meals and accommodation are provided free at the centre.
Step 5: Attend the mandatory placement drives organized by the training partner at the centre — minimum ₹6,000/month salary guaranteed on placement.

schemes.name: Stand-Up India Scheme
schemes.amount: Loan of ₹10 lakh to ₹1 crore per borrower for greenfield enterprise
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply anytime at standupmitra.in or nearest scheduled bank branch
schemes.description: Facilitates bank loans to SC/ST and women entrepreneurs for setting up greenfield enterprises.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Greenfield enterprise (first venture); not a bank defaulter

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to standupmitra.in and click 'Register'.
Step 2: Fill in your personal details and business plan.
Step 3: Connect with a Loan Facilitation Centre (LFC) near you.
Step 4: Alternatively, walk into any scheduled commercial bank and ask for Stand-Up India loan.
Step 5: Submit business documents and project report to the bank.
Step 6: Bank processes and disburses the loan.

schemes.name: Punjab Skill Development Mission (PSDM)
schemes.ministry: Department of Technical Education and Industrial Training, Punjab
schemes.amount: Free skill training + stipend during training (for eligible candidates)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — rolling admissions; visit psdm.gov.in for current batches
schemes.description: State-level skill training mission in Punjab connecting youth to employment through industry-relevant free courses.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Priority to unemployed youth, school dropouts, marginalized communities

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to psdm.gov.in and find a training centre near your location.
Step 2: Register online or visit the centre directly.
Step 3: Choose from available skill courses based on your interest.
Step 4: Complete the training program.
Step 5: Appear for the certification assessment and collect your certificate.

schemes.name: Aam Aadmi Rozgar Yojana (AARY) – Punjab
schemes.amount: Free job portal + free skill courses + job fairs (no direct monetary benefit)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — register anytime at pgrkam.com
schemes.description: Punjab scheme connecting unemployed youth to employment through digital job portal, job fairs, skill training, and entrepreneurship support.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Unemployed graduates and diploma holders registered at Punjab employment exchange

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to pgrkam.com and click 'Register'.
Step 2: Create your profile with education and skill details.
Step 3: Browse available jobs or register for upcoming job fairs.
Step 4: Apply directly to jobs or attend placement drives organized by AARY.

schemes.name: Mukhya Mantri Sehat Bima Yojana (MMSBY) – Punjab
schemes.amount: ₹10 lakh cashless health insurance per family per year (zero premium)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — enroll at any Aam Aadmi Clinic or government hospital in Punjab
schemes.description: Punjab's cashless health insurance covering 100% of Punjab's population for up to ₹10 lakh per family per year.

eligibility.otherCriteria: All Punjab residents covered (100% population)

how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit the nearest Aam Aadmi Clinic (AAC) or government hospital in Punjab — they handle MMSBY enrollment on the spot.
Step 2: Carry your Aadhaar card and ask the hospital staff for MMSBY health card registration.
Step 3: The hospital staff will register your family and issue an MMSBY digital health card.
Step 4: Use the health card for cashless treatment at any MMSBY-empanelled hospital in Punjab — no premium to be paid.
required_documents: Punjab domicile proof or ration card

schemes.name: Merit-cum-Means Scholarship for Minorities
schemes.amount: Course fee up to ₹20,000/year + maintenance allowance ₹10,000/year
schemes.deadline: Annual — apply on NSP by October 31 each year
schemes.description: Financial assistance to students from minority communities pursuing professional/technical courses.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Muslim/Christian/Sikh/Buddhist/Jain/Parsi; 50% marks in previous year; income below ₹2.5 lakh/year

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to scholarships.gov.in and complete registration.
Step 2: Select the minority scholarship scheme for your category.
Step 3: Fill in community certificate, income, and college details.
Step 4: Upload required documents and submit.
Step 5: Institution verifies; Ministry of Minority Affairs releases scholarship.

schemes.name: AICTE Pragati Scholarship for Girls (Technical Education)
schemes.amount: ₹50,000/year (up to 4 years degree / 3 years diploma)
schemes.deadline: Annual — applications open August–October on AICTE portal
schemes.description: Supports girl students from low-income families in AICTE-approved technical institutions to encourage girls in engineering and technical fields.

eligibility.ageLimit: First and second year diploma or degree students
eligibility.otherCriteria: Family income below ₹8 lakh/year; one girl per family per year; AICTE-approved institution

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to aicte-india.org and find the Pragati Scholarship section.
Step 2: Click 'Apply Now' and register with Aadhaar.
Step 3: Fill in your admission year, institution, and family income details.
Step 4: Upload income certificate, admission letter, and mark sheet.
Step 5: Institution verifies; AICTE disburses ₹50,000 annually to bank account.

schemes.name: Ishan Uday Special Scholarship Scheme for NE Region
schemes.amount: ₹5,400–₹7,800/month depending on course type
schemes.deadline: Annual — apply on NSP by October 31 each year
schemes.description: Provides scholarships to students from the North East region of India to pursue higher education in recognized institutions anywhere in India. Encourages NE youth to study outside their home state.

eligibility.residency: Domicile of North East states (Assam, Meghalaya, Manipur, Mizoram, Nagaland, Tripura, Arunachal Pradesh, Sikkim)
eligibility.otherCriteria: Family income below ₹4.5 lakh/year; passed Class 12; pursuing general degree or technical/professional courses

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to scholarships.gov.in and register with Aadhaar.
Step 2: Select 'Ishan Uday Special Scholarship for NE Region'.
Step 3: Fill in your Class 12 details, domicile state, and college details.
Step 4: Upload income certificate, domicile certificate, and admission letter.
Step 5: Your college verifies — UGC releases scholarship to your bank account.

schemes.name: Business Blaster Scheme – Punjab (Student Entrepreneurship)
schemes.amount: Seed money of ₹2,000 per student (up to ₹20,000 per team of 10) as startup capital
schemes.deadline: Annual — runs during academic year; students in Class 11 are automatically enrolled through school
schemes.description: A first-of-its-kind Punjab government initiative giving Class 11 government school students seed money to start their own small business, fostering an entrepreneurial mindset from school itself.

eligibility.residency: Students enrolled in government schools in Punjab
eligibility.otherCriteria: Teams of up to 10 students; must present a viable business idea to school committee

financial: Up to ₹20,000 per team of 10 students
nonFinancial: Mentorship from teachers and industry experts
Top teams featured at state-level competition

how_to_apply: Step 1: This scheme is automatically implemented through Punjab government schools for all Class 11 students — no external application needed.
Step 2: Form a team of up to 10 classmates and develop a viable business idea together.
Step 3: Present your business idea to the school committee appointed under the Business Blaster program.
Step 4: Approved teams receive seed money of ₹2,000 per student (up to ₹20,000 per team of 10).
Step 5: Run your business during the academic year and participate in the state-level Business Blaster competition organized by the Department of School Education, Punjab.
required_documents: School enrollment in Class 11 (government school, Punjab)

schemes.name: National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (NAPS)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
schemes.amount: Government pays ₹1,500/month or 25% of stipend (whichever is less) to employer; apprentice gets full stipend from employer
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — register anytime at apprenticeshipindia.org
schemes.description: Promotes apprenticeship training in establishments by sharing stipend cost with employers, making it easier for youth to get industry experience immediately after Class 10/12 or diploma.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Passed Class 8 minimum; establishment must be registered under NAPS

financial: Stipend from employer (minimum as per government norms)

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to apprenticeshipindia.org and register as an apprentice.
Step 2: Fill in your educational details and preferred trade/sector.
Step 3: Search for registered establishments in your area offering apprenticeships.
Step 4: Apply to suitable establishments and attend selection.
Step 5: Sign the apprenticeship contract and start training — stipend begins immediately.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana – Urban (PMAY-U)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
schemes.amount: Interest subsidy of 3–6.5% on home loan (EWS: 6.5% on ₹6 lakh; MIG-I: 4% on ₹9 lakh; MIG-II: 3% on ₹12 lakh)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing under PMAY 2.0 — apply at pmaymis.gov.in or nearest ULB
schemes.description: Affordable housing for urban EWS/LIG/MIG households through interest subsidies and direct construction support.

eligibility.otherCriteria: No pucca house anywhere; EWS (income ≤₹3 lakh), LIG (₹3–6 lakh), MIG-I (₹6–12 lakh), MIG-II (₹12–18 lakh)

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to pmaymis.gov.in and click 'Citizen Assessment'.
Step 2: Check eligibility by entering your Aadhaar number.
Step 3: Fill in personal, income, and property details.
Step 4: Submit application through the portal or visit the nearest Urban Local Body (ULB).
Step 5: Alternatively, apply through any scheduled bank for the CLSS interest subsidy.
Step 6: After approval, interest subsidy is credited upfront to reduce home loan EMI.
required_documents: Aadhaar card of applicant and spouse
optional_documents: Property purchase agreement (if house already selected)
Affidavit of no pucca house ownership

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana – Gramin (PMAY-G)
schemes.amount: ₹1,20,000 (plains) or ₹1,30,000 (hilly areas) + ₹12,000 for toilet under SBM
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — eligibility based on SECC 2011 waitlist; contact Gram Panchayat
schemes.description: Rural housing scheme providing financial assistance to BPL households to construct pucca houses with basic amenities.

eligibility.otherCriteria: SECC 2011 identified; houseless or kutcha house

how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit your Gram Panchayat office and request the Village Development Officer (VDO) or Gram Sachiv to check if your name is in the PMAY-G SECC 2011 permanent waitlist.
Step 2: If your name is listed, fill the PMAY-G application form at the Gram Panchayat.
Step 3: The Block Development Officer (BDO) verifies your application and adds you to the beneficiary list.
Step 4: Funds are transferred in instalments (₹40,000 → ₹40,000 → ₹40,000) based on construction progress verified by the BDO.
Step 5: Each construction stage is confirmed via geo-tagged photos taken through the official AwaasSoft mobile app by the local authority.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY)
schemes.amount: Zero balance account + ₹2 lakh accidental insurance + ₹30,000 life insurance + ₹10,000 overdraft
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — open account at any bank branch or Business Correspondent anytime
schemes.description: Universal banking access with zero balance accounts, free insurance, overdraft facility, and access to government benefits.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Primarily for unbanked individuals

how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit any bank branch or the nearest Business Correspondent (BC) / Banking Mitra in your area.
Step 2: Ask the bank teller or BC for the PMJDY (Jan Dhan) account opening form.
Step 3: Fill in your name, Aadhaar number, and nominee information on the form.
Step 4: Submit your Aadhaar card and one passport-size photograph.
Step 5: Receive your PMJDY passbook and a RuPay debit card — your zero-balance account is active with ₹2 lakh accidental insurance.
optional_documents: Voter ID / Driving License / NREGA Job Card (if Aadhaar not available)

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
schemes.amount: ₹2 lakh life insurance cover at only ₹436/year premium
schemes.deadline: Annual renewal — auto-debited on June 1; enroll anytime at your bank
schemes.description: Government-backed term life insurance providing ₹2 lakh on death at a nominal ₹436/year premium.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Must have bank account with auto-debit facility

financial: ₹2 lakh life cover on death (any cause)

how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit your bank branch or use your bank's net banking or mobile banking app.
Step 2: Ask the bank teller for PMJJBY enrollment, or find the option in your banking app under 'Insurance'.
Step 3: Fill the consent-cum-declaration form for auto-debit of ₹436 per year from your savings account.
Step 4: Add your nominee's name and details on the form.
Step 5: Submit the form at the branch — your policy activates from June 1 of the enrollment year (₹2 lakh life cover).

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
schemes.amount: ₹2 lakh on accidental death/full disability; ₹1 lakh on partial disability — at just ₹20/year
schemes.deadline: Annual renewal — auto-debited on June 1; enroll anytime at your bank
schemes.description: Very low-cost accidental insurance providing ₹2 lakh cover for accidental death and disability at just ₹20 per year.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Must have bank account

financial: ₹2 lakh on accidental death or total disability

how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit your bank branch or use your bank's net banking or mobile banking app.
Step 2: Ask for the PMSBY enrollment form at the counter, or find the option in your banking app under 'Insurance'.
Step 3: Fill the consent form for auto-debit of ₹20 per year from your savings account.
Step 4: Add your nominee's details.
Step 5: Submit the form at the branch — your policy activates from June 1 (₹2 lakh accidental death cover at just ₹20/year).

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat – PM Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
schemes.amount: ₹5 lakh cashless health insurance per family per year (zero premium)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — check eligibility and get Ayushman card at beneficiary.nha.gov.in or empanelled hospital
schemes.description: World's largest government health insurance scheme providing ₹5 lakh/year for secondary and tertiary care to crores of families.

eligibility.otherCriteria: SECC 2011 identified families; recently extended to all senior citizens above 70 years

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to beneficiary.nha.gov.in to check if your name is on the beneficiary list.
Step 2: Enter your Aadhaar number or ration card number to verify eligibility.
Step 3: If eligible, visit the nearest empanelled government hospital or Ayushman Bharat Mitra.
Step 4: Get your Ayushman Bharat Golden Card (e-card) made with Aadhaar.
Step 5: Use the card for cashless treatment at any empanelled hospital across India.
optional_documents: Ration card or SECC inclusion proof

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
schemes.amount: Free LPG connection (cylinder + regulator + hose); continued subsidy via PAHAL
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply at nearest LPG distributor anytime
schemes.description: Provides free LPG connections to women from BPL households to replace polluting cooking fuels.

eligibility.otherCriteria: BPL/SECC/SC/ST/forest area/PMAY/PMJDY/tea garden workers

financial: Free LPG connection with cylinder, regulator, hose

how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit the nearest LPG gas agency in your area (HP Gas, Bharat Gas, or Indane — whichever serves your locality).
Step 2: Ask the agency counter for the Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) KYC form.
Step 3: Fill in the woman applicant's name, Aadhaar details, and address.
Step 4: Submit the BPL / SECC certificate (or relevant eligibility proof) and Aadhaar card at the agency.
Step 5: The gas agency verifies your application and submits it to the oil company.
Step 6: The free gas connection (cylinder + regulator + hose) is delivered to your home — subsequent refills are subsidized via PAHAL DBT.
required_documents: Address proof (Aadhaar address or utility bill)
optional_documents: BPL ration card or SECC identification

schemes.name: MGNREGS (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee)
schemes.amount: 100 days guaranteed employment/year at ₹220–₹357/day (state-wise wage rate)
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply for Job Card at Gram Panchayat anytime; work within 15 days
schemes.description: Guarantees 100 days of wage employment per year to every rural household whose adult members volunteer for unskilled manual work.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Job Card required; any rural adult willing to do unskilled manual work

financial: Unemployment allowance if work not given in 15 days

how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit your Gram Panchayat office and ask for the MGNREGS Job Card registration form.
Step 2: Fill in the form with the name and Aadhaar details of all adult members of your household.
Step 3: Submit photographs of all adult members along with your Aadhaar cards at the Gram Panchayat.
Step 4: Receive your MGNREGS Job Card within 15 days of application.
Step 5: Whenever you need work, submit a written application to the Gram Panchayat requesting employment.
Step 6: The Gram Panchayat must provide work within 15 days; wages are paid within 15 days of work completion directly into your bank account.
required_documents: Aadhaar card of all adult members
Bank or post office account details
optional_documents: Rural address proof (if Aadhaar address is different)

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.amount: Full sum insured for crop loss; farmer pays only 2% (Kharif), 1.5% (Rabi), 5% (horticulture)
schemes.deadline: Seasonal — Kharif: apply by July 31; Rabi: apply by December 31
schemes.description: Comprehensive crop insurance against natural calamities, pests, and diseases for financial stability of farmers.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Loanee and non-loanee farmers; sharecroppers and tenant farmers eligible

financial: Full sum insured for total crop loss
Farmer premium only 2% Kharif, 1.5% Rabi, 5% horticulture

how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit the nearest bank, Common Service Centre (CSC), or insurance company agent before the cut-off date.
Step 2: Fill the crop insurance application with your crop and land details.
Step 3: Pay the applicable premium (very low — only 2% for Kharif).
Step 4: Claim is automatically settled based on crop cutting experiments and satellite data.
Alternatively: Apply online at pmfby.gov.in
required_documents: Land ownership records (Khasra/Khatauni) or rental agreement

schemes.name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.amount: ₹6,000/year in 3 instalments of ₹2,000 every 4 months via DBT
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — register anytime at pmkisan.gov.in
schemes.description: Direct income support of ₹6,000/year to all eligible farmer families in 3 instalments.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Small and marginal farmers with arable land; excludes government employees, income tax payers

financial: Direct credit to Aadhaar-linked bank account

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to pmkisan.gov.in and click 'Farmers Corner'.
Step 2: Click 'New Farmer Registration' and enter your Aadhaar number.
Step 3: Fill in your name, land details, state, and bank account number.
Step 4: Submit the form — Patwari will verify your land records.
Step 5: After verification, ₹2,000 instalments start coming to your bank account every 4 months.
Alternatively: Visit nearest CSC (Common Service Centre) for assisted registration.

schemes.name: DAY-NULM (National Urban Livelihoods Mission)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
schemes.amount: Individual loan subsidy up to ₹2 lakh; group up to ₹10 lakh; SHG Revolving Fund ₹10,000
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — contact nearest Urban Local Body (ULB) or City Livelihood Centre
schemes.description: Reduces urban poverty through skill development, SHG formation, and credit linkage for urban poor.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Urban poor, rag pickers, street vendors, construction workers

financial: Interest subsidy on loans up to ₹2 lakh (individual)
Group enterprise loan up to ₹10 lakh

how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit the nearest Urban Local Body (ULB) office such as the Municipal Corporation, Municipal Council, or City Livelihood Centre in your city.
Step 2: Ask the DAY-NULM program officer about registration for skill training or Self-Help Group (SHG) formation.
Step 3: For individual skill training — fill the registration form and submit documents at the ULB.
Step 4: For group loans — join or form a Self-Help Group (SHG) at the City Livelihood Centre and collectively apply for the SHG Revolving Fund or group loan.
Step 5: After verification by the ULB officer, skill training placement or loan disbursement is arranged.

schemes.name: PM SVANidhi (Street Vendor Micro-Credit)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
schemes.amount: Initial ₹10,000 → ₹20,000 → ₹50,000 on timely repayment + 7% interest subsidy
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply anytime at pmsvanidhi.mohua.gov.in
schemes.description: Collateral-free working capital loans for urban street vendors with escalating credit limits on timely repayment.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Certificate of Vending or ULB Letter of Recommendation; surveyed vendor

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to pmsvanidhi.mohua.gov.in and click 'Apply for Loan'.
Step 2: Enter your Aadhaar number and mobile number.
Step 3: Fill in your vending details and location.
Step 4: Upload your Certificate of Vending or visit ULB for Letter of Recommendation.
Step 5: Select a lending institution (bank or MFI) and apply for the loan.
Step 6: Loan is disbursed; repay on time to get higher loan next round.
required_documents: Certificate of Vending or ULB Letter of Recommendation
optional_documents: Digital payment app setup (for cashback benefits)

schemes.name: Sukh Samman Nidhi Scheme – Punjab (Women Support)
schemes.ministry: Department of Social Security and Women & Child Development, Punjab
schemes.amount: ₹1,000/month directly credited to bank account
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — apply at sswcd.punjab.gov.in or nearest Sewa Kendra
schemes.description: Monthly ₹1,000 financial support to women above 18 from low-income Punjab families to enhance financial independence.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Family income below ₹3 lakh/year; not a government employee or income tax payer

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to sswcd.punjab.gov.in or visit the nearest Sewa Kendra or DC office.
Step 2: Ask for the Sukh Samman Nidhi application form.
Step 3: Fill in your personal details, income, and bank account number.
Step 4: Submit the form with income certificate and Aadhaar.
Step 5: After verification, monthly ₹1,000 starts being credited to your bank account.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Rozgar Protsahan Yojana (PMRPY)
schemes.amount: Government pays full 12% employer EPF contribution for 3 years per new employee
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — employer registers new employees on EPFO portal; benefit is automatic
schemes.description: Encourages formal hiring by having government pay full employer EPF contribution for new employees earning below ₹15,000/month.

eligibility.ageLimit: New employees of any age; salary below ₹15,000/month
eligibility.otherCriteria: First-time EPFO registrant; employer must be EPFO-registered

financial: 12% employer EPF contribution paid by government for 3 years

how_to_apply: Step 1: Employer registers with EPFO at epfindia.gov.in.
Step 2: Hire new employees with salary below ₹15,000/month.
Step 3: Register new employees on the EPFO portal as first-time EPFO members.
Step 4: Government automatically pays the employer's 12% EPF contribution for 3 years.
Step 5: Employee benefits by getting EPFO social security coverage from Day 1.

schemes.name: Employment Linked Incentive (ELI) – PM Viksit Bharat Rozgar Yojana
schemes.amount: Scheme A: ₹15,000 one-month salary for first-time employees; Scheme B: ₹3,000/month for manufacturing employers for 2 years
schemes.deadline: Scheme approved July 2025 — rolling implementation; check labour.gov.in
schemes.description: Landmark ₹2 lakh crore employment scheme creating formal jobs for 4.1 crore youth through salary support and employer incentives.

eligibility.otherCriteria: First-time EPFO registrant for Scheme A; manufacturing sector for Scheme B

financial: Scheme A: One-month salary up to ₹15,000 in 3 instalments
Scheme B: Up to ₹3,000/month reimbursement to manufacturing employers for 2 years
Scheme C: Reimbursement for employers adding 1,000+ new jobs

how_to_apply: Step 1: For employees (Scheme A) — join a formal sector company and register with EPFO for the first time.
Step 2: Your employer registers you on the EPFO portal as a new member.
Step 3: Government directly transfers Scheme A benefit (up to ₹15,000) to your Aadhaar-linked bank account in 3 instalments.
Step 4: Complete a mandatory financial literacy course before receiving the second instalment.
Step 5: Manufacturing employers apply through labour.gov.in for Scheme B reimbursement.
required_documents: Aadhaar card of employee (Aadhaar-linked bank account)
optional_documents: Bank account opening proof (for new EPFO members)

schemes.name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
schemes.amount: Guaranteed pension of ₹1,000–₹5,000/month after age 60; government co-contributes 50% or ₹1,000/year (whichever is lower) for non-income-tax payers
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — enroll at any bank between 18 and 40 years of age
schemes.description: A guaranteed minimum pension scheme for workers in the unorganized sector, ensuring financial security after retirement for those aged 60 and above.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Must have savings bank account; not covered under statutory social security schemes; not an income tax payer (for government co-contribution)

financial: Government co-contributes 50% or ₹1,000/year (for non-IT payers in first 5 years)
Return of corpus to spouse/nominee on death

how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit any bank where you have a savings account.
Step 2: Ask for the APY enrollment form.
Step 3: Fill in your contribution level (select desired pension amount ₹1,000–₹5,000).
Step 4: Submit Aadhaar, mobile number, and bank account details.
Step 5: Monthly contribution is auto-debited from your account.
Alternatively: Enroll through net banking or mobile banking of your bank.

schemes.name: e-Shram Card Scheme
schemes.amount: ₹2 lakh accidental insurance (under PMSBY) + access to all future government welfare schemes
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — register anytime at eshram.gov.in (free registration)
schemes.description: A national database of unorganized workers providing them a unique 12-digit e-Shram Card. Registered workers get accidental insurance and access to all social security and welfare schemes.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Unorganized sector workers (construction workers, street vendors, domestic workers, agricultural laborers, gig workers, etc.); not EPFO/ESIC member

financial: ₹2 lakh accidental insurance (free for first year)
Access to all future government welfare schemes through single card

how_to_apply: Step 1: Go to eshram.gov.in and click 'Register on e-Shram'.
Step 2: Enter your Aadhaar number and mobile number (OTP verification).
Step 3: Fill in your occupation, work type, bank details, and address.
Step 4: Submit the form — e-Shram Card is generated instantly (downloadable as PDF).
Alternatively: Visit nearest CSC (Common Service Centre) for free assisted registration.

schemes.name: PM Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana (PMGKAY)
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Food and Public Distribution
schemes.amount: 5 kg free grain (rice/wheat) per person per month in addition to NFSA quota
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — benefits provided automatically through ration card; scheme extended till 2028
schemes.description: Provides 5 kg of free food grain per person per month to 80 crore NFSA beneficiaries, ensuring food security for the poorest families.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Must be covered under NFSA (National Food Security Act) — Priority Household (PHH) or Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) ration card

financial: 5 kg free grain (rice/wheat) per person per month
In addition to existing NFSA quota — total up to 10 kg/month per person

how_to_apply: Step 1: No separate application is needed — benefits are automatic for NFSA ration card holders (PHH or AAY category).
Step 2: Visit your nearest Fair Price Shop (FPS / ration shop) on the designated distribution day each month.
Step 3: Provide biometric authentication (Aadhaar fingerprint) at the PoS machine at the ration shop.
Step 4: Collect your free 5 kg grain per person per month (rice/wheat) from the shop.
Step 5: If you do not have an NFSA ration card, visit your state's Food and Civil Supplies Department office to apply for one.
required_documents: Aadhaar card (for biometric verification at ration shop)
optional_documents: Aadhaar card of all family members (for One Nation One Ration Card portability)

schemes.name: Punjab Maternity Benefit Scheme
schemes.amount: ₹36,000 paid in 3 instalments during pregnancy and post-delivery
schemes.deadline: Ongoing — register at district labour office or nearest Seva Kendra within 1 year of pregnancy
schemes.description: Provides financial assistance to registered building and construction workers in Punjab during maternity. Also extends to unorganized sector women workers for pre- and post-natal care.

eligibility.otherCriteria: Registered with Punjab Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (BOCW); or eligible under PMMVY nationally

financial: ₹36,000 in 3 instalments (₹12,000 each during pregnancy and post-delivery)

how_to_apply: Step 1: First, register with the Punjab Building and Other Construction Workers (BOCW) Welfare Board — visit the nearest district labour office in Punjab for BOCW registration.
Step 2: After pregnancy confirmation, visit the nearest district labour office or the nearest Seva Kendra in Punjab.
Step 3: Ask for the Punjab Maternity Benefit Scheme application form and fill it completely.
Step 4: Submit the form with all required documents — a labour welfare officer will verify your BOCW registration and pregnancy proof.
Step 5: Three instalments of ₹12,000 each are credited directly to your bank account at different stages of pregnancy and post-delivery.
